pr.title: [WIP] Create static website for Attack Your Rights (AYR)
pr.body:
- [x] Inspect repository contents and identify existing build/test infrastructure
- [x] Confirm there is no existing lint/build/test setup to run before changes
- [x] Add global accessible stylesheet (`style.css`) aligned with WCAG-oriented design constraints
- [x] Create semantic pages: `index.html`, `resources.html`, `blog.html`, `blog-post-template.html`, and `contact.html`
- [x] Ensure each page includes responsive metadata, SEO basics, accessible landmarks/navigation, and realistic starter content
- [x] Manually validate page rendering and accessibility-focused structure; capture UI screenshot(s)
- [ ] Run any available targeted validation (if present), then finalize with security/review validation

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

# SYSTEM PROMPT: STATIC WEBSITE GENERATION - ATTACK YOUR RIGHTS (AYR)

You are an expert Frontend Developer, Accessibility (WCAG/ADA) Specialist, and UX Designer. Your goal is to scaffold and build a lightweight, secure, and production-ready static website named **Attack Your Rights (AYR)** based on the specifications below.

---

## 1. MISSION & TARGET AUDIENCE OVERVIEW
*   **The Mission:** AYR is dedicated to bringing awareness to human rights defenders (HRDs), their missions, and related technical resources. It hosts actionable cybersecurity content, defensive tools, and threat intelligence breakdowns.
*   **The Content:** 
    *   Technical blog posts focusing on operational security (OpSec) for HRDs, journalists, and activists operating in hostile environments with low bandwidth or minimal funding.
    *   Practical translations of threat intel reports (e.g., Citizen Lab, Amnesty International, AccessNow) into actionable defensive assets: Detection rules (YARA, Sigma, KQL), automation scripts, and hardened configuration files.
*   **The Constraints:** The target audience often uses older hardware, suffers from low bandwidth, or accesses the site via high-latency networks (TOR, VPNs). The site **must** be blazing fast, lightweight, and rely on zero tracking or heavy JS frameworks.

---

## 2. TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE & STACK
*   **Platform:** GitHub Pages (`username.github.io/repo-name`).
*   **Stack:** Pure Semantic HTML5, Vanilla CSS3 (Modern/Minimalist), and zero or minimal Vanilla JavaScript (only if strictly necessary for UI toggle features). No heavy frameworks (No React, No Angular).
*   **Accessibility (ADA/WCAG Compliance):** 
    *   Must meet **WCAG 2.1 AA** standards at a minimum.
    *   High color contrast ratios (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text).
    *   Full keyboard navigability (`tabindex`, visible focus states like `outline: 2px solid`).
    *   Semantic landmarks (`<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<article>`, `<footer>`).
    *   Screen-reader friendly (`aria-labels` where appropriate, explicit `<label>` tags for all form fields, alt text placeholders).

---

## 3. DESIGN & DESIGN SYSTEM (CSS)
Create a single, clean, cohesive global stylesheet (`style.css`) that reflects a modern, stark, and highly legible aesthetic:
*   **Theme/Vibe:** Minimalist, clean, authoritative, yet human-centric. 
*   **Color Palette (Accessible Archetype):**
    *   Background: Clean off-white or light gray (`#F9F9F9`) to reduce eye strain.
    *   Primary Text: High-contrast dark charcoal (`#1A1A1A`).
    *   Accent/Alert Color: A distinct, meaningful color (e.g., Deep Crimson or Security Blue) used sparingly for links, buttons, and interactive states.
*   **Typography:** System fonts for speed and privacy (No external Google Fonts calls to avoid tracking). Use: `system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`.
*   **Layout:** Fully responsive Flexbox or CSS Grid that scales seamlessly from small mobile screens to widescreen desktops.

---

## 4. ARCHITECTURE & SITE LAYOUT
Generate the following file structure and contents:

### Page 1: Home Page (`index.html`)
*   **Header & Navigation:** Clean text logo ("Attack Your Rights") with a visible, accessible nav bar linking to all pages.
*   **Hero Section:** A powerful, clear statement outlining the site's mission to support civil society and human rights defense.
*   **Global Crisis Dashboard/Feed Section:** A clean layout presenting general information on ongoing human rights advocacy and defense crises across the globe.
*   **Recent Content Highlights:** A section pulling or listing the latest 2-3 blog posts or tools.

### Page 2: Tools & Resources (`resources.html`)
*   **Categorized Directory:** A clean, searchable, or filterable list layout for external links, civil society organizations, and open-source defensive tools.
*   **Required Categories:**
    1. *Civil Society Organizations* (Links to AccessNow, Citizen Lab, Front Line Defenders, etc.).
    2. *Personal Security & OpSec Tools* (Signal, Tor, encrypted storage, etc.).
    3. *Threat Intel & Defensive Assets* (Where detection rules, Sigma configs, and scripts live or link out to inside the repo).

### Page 3: Blog Index (`blog.html`)
*   **Article List:** A clean chronological feed of articles. Each entry needs a Title, Publication Date, Read Time, Category Tag, and a short, compelling Excerpt.
*   **Post Layout Template (`blog-post-template.html`):** A blueprint for individual articles. It must feature:
    *   An `<article>` wrapper with structured typographic hierarchy (`<h1>` down to `<h3>`).
    *   Styling for `<code>` elements and `<pre>` blocks to display detection rules (YARA, Sigma, KQL) cleanly with a monospaced font.
    *   Callout boxes or blockquotes (`<blockquote>`) for urgent OpSec tips or threat model warnings.

### Page 4: Contact & Submissions (`contact.html`)
*   **Objective:** Provide a secure, straightforward way fo...

</details>
pr.author: Copilot
